new_classes.definition_en: A liquid condiment produced by acetic fermentation of an alcoholic or other substrate, classified under heading 2209 of the Combined Nomenclature.
Vinegar obtained exclusively by acetic fermentation of wine, classified under CN subheadings 2209 00 11 and 2209 00 19 depending on container size.

Wine vinegar presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2209 00 11. See additional note 11 to Chapter 22 and the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I), second paragraph, (1).
A biological process in which ethanol is oxidised by acetic acid bacteria to produce acetic acid (vinegar), distinguishing vinegar from other fermented beverages in Chapter 22.
Wine of fresh grapes used as the substrate for acetic fermentation to produce wine vinegar, as referred to in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I).

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2209 00 11: (1) the product is wine vinegar (produced from wine by acetic fermentation), and (2) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L (SmallContainer). The AceticFermentationProcess class with WineFeedstock part models the wine-origin requirement. Coverage is partial (0.75) because: (a) the legal note cross-references 'additional note 11 to this chapter', whose precise content is not available in the provided legal text and cannot be fully axiomatised; (b) the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I), second paragraph, (1) may contain further product-discriminating criteria (e.g. minimum acidity, absence of other vinegars) that cannot be modelled without the full note text. The sibling code 22090019 (wine vinegar in containers > 2 L) is distinguished by the LargeContainer complement restriction already implied. No regulatory individual codes (MeasureType, Footnote, Certificate) appear in the running TBox that can be referenced for this code.
